children.label: A
children.text: establishing legal rights and procedures for their prompt return; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensuring the enforcement of reciprocal international obligations under the Hague Abduction Convention or arrangements under bilateral procedures;
to promote the timely resolution of abduction cases through 1 or more of the actions described in ; andsection 9122 of this title
to ensure appropriate coordination within the Federal Government and between Federal, State, and local agencies involved in abduction prevention, investigation, and resolution.

children.heading: Actions by the Secretary of State in response to unresolved cases
children.label: b Actions by the Secretary of State in response to unresolved cases

children.heading: Determination of action by the Secretary of State
children.label: 1 Determination of action by the Secretary of State
A
children.text: take 1 or more of the actions described in subsections (d) and (e) of ; andsection 9122 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: direct the Chief of Mission in that foreign country to directly address the resolution of the case with senior officials in the foreign government.

children.heading: Authority for delay of action by the Secretary of State
children.label: 2 Authority for delay of action by the Secretary of State
children.text: The Secretary of State may delay any action described in paragraph (1) if the Secretary determines that an additional period of time, not to exceed 1 year, will substantially assist in resolving the case.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: If the Secretary of State delays any action pursuant to paragraph (2) or decides not to take an action described in subsection (d) or (e) of after making the determination described in paragraph (1), the Secretary, not later than 15 days after such delay or decision, shall provide a report to the appropriate congressional committees that details the reasons for delaying action or not taking action, as appropriate.section 9122 of this title

children.heading: Congressional briefings
children.label: 4 Congressional briefings
children.text: At the request of the appropriate congressional committees, the Secretary of State shall provide a detailed briefing, including a written report, if requested, on actions taken to resolve a case or the cause for delay.

children.label: A
children.text: take 1 or more actions that most appropriately respond to the nature and severity of the governmental failure to resolve the unresolved abduction case; and

children.label: i
children.text: to initially respond by communicating with the Central Authority of the country; and

children.label: I
children.text: as narrowly as practicable, with respect to the agencies or instrumentalities of the foreign government that are responsible for such failures; and

children.label: II
children.text: in ways that respect the separation of powers and independence of the judiciary of the country, as applicable.

children.heading: Guidelines for actions by the Secretary of State
children.label: 2 Guidelines for actions by the Secretary of State
A
children.text: the population of the country whose government is targeted by the action or actions;

children.label: B
children.text: the humanitarian activities of United States and nongovernmental organizations in the country; and

children.label: C
children.text: the national security interests of the United States.

children.heading: Action in the case of abducted children who reach the age of 16
children.label: d Action in the case of abducted children who reach the age of 16
children.text: When an individual who is an abducted child attains 16 years of age, a consular officer from a United States diplomatic mission in the country in which such individual resides shall, until either the left-behind parent seeking assistance or the individual (after attaining 18 years of age) requests the officer to cease, annually attempt to contact such individual, through welfare and whereabout visits and by engaging other agencies and foreign counterparts as necessary, to provide information, as relevant, on rights and privileges as a United States citizen, such as passports, and any eligible benefits from left-behind parent, such as G.I. educational and health benefits and to obtain a verified location of such individual.
